title: Buttondown Review 2025: Features, Pricing & Honest Verdict
meta_description: Is Buttondown worth it in 2025? We break down pricing, features, pros and cons so you can decide.
h1: Buttondown Review 2025: Everything You Need to Know
intro: Buttondown is the best way to write a newsletter that positions itself for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown. Founded in 2017, it has become one of the most recognised platforms in the email marketing space. In this review we cover what it does well, where it falls short, and who should actually use it.
verdict: Buttondown is a solid choice for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown. Its free tier is genuinely useful. The main trade-off is between its particular strengths and the limitations that make alternatives worth considering for some users.
pros: Good fit for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown
cons: Can get expensive at higher subscriber counts
Some features locked to higher tiers
pricing_summary: Buttondown offers a free plan, with paid plans from $9/month. Pricing typically scales with subscriber count.
best_for_detail: Buttondown works best for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown. If you fit that profile, the feature set aligns well. If you need something different, check the alternatives section.

faq.a: Yes, Buttondown has a free plan supporting up to 100 subscribers. This makes it accessible for new senders testing the platform.

faq.q: What is Buttondown best used for?
faq.a: Buttondown is best for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown. Its standout features include markdown writing, API access, paid subscriptions, which suit this use case well.
Buttondown is generally beginner-friendly. The interface is straightforward for basic campaigns, though advanced automation features have a learning curve.

faq.q: Does Buttondown have an affiliate program?
faq.a: Buttondown does not currently offer an affiliate program. This is worth considering if you plan to recommend the platform to your audience.
